name: Maternal haematocrit at term
tier_rationale: Tier B pending re-review. Source citation: hytten-chamberlain-1980-blood-volume. Technique: Microhaematocrit centrifugation. Population: Healthy singleton pregnancies, term.
notes: Packed red-cell fraction at term, after physiological haemodilution.

name: Maternal haemoglobin concentration at term
tier_rationale: Tier B pending re-review. Source citation: hytten-chamberlain-1980-blood-volume. Technique: Cyanmethaemoglobin spectrophotometry. Population: Healthy singleton pregnancies, term. Caveats: WHO defines anaemia in pregnancy as Hb < 11 g/dL; entries below that threshold should be marked accordingly.
notes: Whole-blood haemoglobin at term. Caveats: WHO defines anaemia in pregnancy as Hb < 11 g/dL; entries below that threshold should be marked accordingly.

name: Hill coefficient for maternal oxygen-haemoglobin binding
tier_rationale: Tier A pending re-review. Source citation: severinghaus-1979-o2-hb-dissociation. Technique: Polarographic determination of oxygen saturation as a function of partial pressure. Population: Adult human blood at standard pH and temperature. Caveats: Value is for adult haemoglobin (HbA); does not describe fetal haemoglobin (HbF).

applicability.population: Adult human blood at standard pH and temperature

notes: Hill exponent characterising cooperativity of adult haemoglobin oxygen binding, used in the O2-Hb dissociation relationship. Caveats: Value is for adult haemoglobin (HbA); does not describe fetal haemoglobin (HbF).

name: P50 for maternal oxygen-haemoglobin dissociation
tier_rationale: Tier A pending re-review. Source citation: severinghaus-1979-o2-hb-dissociation. Technique: Polarographic determination of oxygen saturation as a function of partial pressure. Population: Adult human blood at pH 7.4, 37°C. Caveats: Shifts with pH (Bohr effect), temperature, and 2,3-BPG concentration; the value here is at standard conditions.
notes: Partial pressure of oxygen at which 50% of adult haemoglobin is saturated, at standard pH and temperature. Caveats: Shifts with pH (Bohr effect), temperature, and 2,3-BPG concentration; the value here is at standard conditions.

name: Bohr coefficient (Δlog P50 per ΔpH)
tier_rationale: Tier A pending re-review. Source citation: dash-bassingthwaighte-2010-o2-hb. Technique: In-vitro pH titration with simultaneous oxygen saturation. Population: Adult human blood, in-vitro tonometry. Caveats: Bohr slope is itself mildly pH-dependent; a single coefficient is an adequate first-order model.
notes: Slope of P50 versus pH on a log–linear axis. Used to translate maternal-fetal pH differences into a leftward fetal O2 dissociation shift. Caveats: Bohr slope is itself mildly pH-dependent; a single coefficient is an adequate first-order model.

name: Maternal plasma volume in early pregnancy
tier_rationale: Tier B pending re-review. Source citation: bernstein-ziegler-2001-plasma-volume. Technique: Evans-blue indicator dilution. Population: Healthy singleton pregnancies, weeks 12-14.
notes: Plasma volume at the end of the first trimester, recording the early rise documented by Bernstein 2001.

name: Maternal plasma volume at term
tier_rationale: Tier B pending re-review. Source citation: de-haas-2017-plasma-volume-meta. Technique: Systematic review pooling Evans-blue and isotope-dilution studies. Population: Healthy singleton pregnancies.
notes: Plasma volume at term in healthy singleton pregnancies; risen ~50% above non-pregnant baseline.

name: Maternal red-cell mass at term
tier_rationale: Tier B pending re-review. Source citation: hytten-chamberlain-1980-blood-volume. Technique: 51Cr-labelled red-cell dilution as compiled in Hytten & Chamberlain. Population: Healthy singleton pregnancies, term.
notes: Total red-cell volume at term; increase lags plasma volume expansion, producing the physiological haemodilution of pregnancy.

name: Maternal total blood volume
tier_rationale: Tier B pending re-review. Source citation: hytten-chamberlain-1980-blood-volume. Technique: Indicator-dilution measurements compiled in Hytten & Chamberlain. Population: Healthy singleton pregnancies, term. Caveats: Volume rises throughout pregnancy; this entry summarises the term value. Trimester-resolved entries should be added when finer data is wired in.
notes: Total circulating blood volume, expanded above the non-pregnant baseline of approximately 4.5 L. Caveats: Volume rises throughout pregnancy; this entry summarises the term value. Trimester-resolved entries should be added when finer data is wired in.
